activation_results.reason: Exact match of the skill's canonical use case 'Calculate unit economics'.
Paraphrase swapping Calculate for Estimate still clearly targets unit economics calculation.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to unit economics.
Meta-question about skills, not a request to calculate unit economics.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Uses AUD currency, Brisbane/QLD locale, 'labour' (not 'labor') multiple times in CAC/contribution sections.

criterion_results.text: Calculation steps are shown; inputs and assumptions are explicit
criterion_results.evidence: LTV table shows inputs/source ($3,200 x 0.68 = $2,176; x32 months = $69,632; DCF-adjusted $38,400); CAC breakdown shows spend/clients/CAC per channel and fully-loaded build-up.
Has Core Metrics Summary, CAC Breakdown, LTV Calculation, Contribution Margin Waterfall, Scenario Analysis, Cohort Performance, Strategic Narrative and Action Items.

judge_result.notes: Artefact closely matches what the skill description promises: CAC, LTV, payback period, contribution margin and scenario analysis for a service business, with Australian-English narrative and explicit inputs. The third criterion text is truncated in the prompt (no specific section list provided), so it is judged met against the standard canonical unit-economics sections which are all present. Verdict marked partial only because the third criterion is incompletely specified in the prompt; on the substance presented, all criteria are satisfied.
